page: Microsoft Azure F80alds_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Faldsv7 family, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 80 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with a base frequency of 4.5 GHz. The system is configured with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, providing 9449 GB of local disk capacity with Premium Storage capabilities. No GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, as well as PassMark CPU and memory metrics. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 14268.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 477.11 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform exceptionally well, with single-threaded gzip compression at 41.03 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 1071.68 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, with Redis achieving 204.83 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the F80alds_v7 is its low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, which makes it less suitable for memory-bound workloads despite its massive compute capacity. However, from a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of 80 dedicated physical cores combined with nearly 9.5 TB of local storage offers a consolidated platform for compute-heavy and storage-intensive tasks without the overhead of external storage attachments. This configuration is optimized for compute-heavy workloads, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where it demonstrates strong to top-tier performance even on larger 70B parameter models.
description: The Microsoft Azure F80alds_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the Faldsv7 family, featuring 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 160.0 GB of system memory, representing a low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 9449 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier performance across CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmarks, including Redis SET operations and static web serving. While the low memory allocation presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, the high core count and substantial local storage provide qualitative cost efficiency for compute-dense tasks. This server is suited for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, compression tasks, and CPU-based large language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F80alds_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 80 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 160 GB RAM, and 9449 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier database and CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F80alds_v7 is an 80-core AMD EPYC compute-optimized server with 160 GB RAM and 9449 GB local storage for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, 80-core compute-optimized platform with massive local storage for demanding database, compression, and CPU-intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
160.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
9449 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Suited for high-throughput database operations, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
